input.__tool_use_purpose: Verify the change is actually in the file
Check what just serve does
Full serve recipe
Create a minimal html/template repro to confirm behavior
input.command:
cat > /tmp/tpl_test.go <<'EOF'
package main

import (
	"html/template"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	tpl := template.Must(template.New("t").Parse(`<script id="d" type="application/json">{{.J}}</script>`))
	j := `{"a":1,"b":"x"}`
	tpl.Execute(os.Stdout, map[string]any{"J": j})
	os.Stdout.WriteString("\n---\n")
	tpl.Execute(os.Stdout, map[string]any{"J": template.HTML(j)})
	os.Stdout.WriteString("\n---\n")
	tpl.Execute(os.Stdout, map[string]any{"J": template.JS(j)})
	os.Stdout.WriteString("\n")
}
EOF
go run /tmp/tpl_test.go
rm /tmp/tpl_test.go

input.__tool_use_purpose: Rename file and test
input.command:
cat > /tmp/tpl_repro.go <<'EOF'
package main

import (
	"html/template"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	tpl := template.Must(template.New("t").Parse(`<script id="d" type="application/json">{{.J}}</script>`))
	j := `{"a":1,"b":"x"}`
	tpl.Execute(os.Stdout, map[string]any{"J": j})
	os.Stdout.WriteString("\n---\n")
	tpl.Execute(os.Stdout, map[string]any{"J": template.HTML(j)})
	os.Stdout.WriteString("\n---\n")
	tpl.Execute(os.Stdout, map[string]any{"J": template.JS(j)})
	os.Stdout.WriteString("\n---\n")
	tpl.Execute(os.Stdout, map[string]any{"J": template.JSStr(j)})
	os.Stdout.WriteString("\n")
}
EOF
go run /tmp/tpl_repro.go
rm /tmp/tpl_repro.go

input.__tool_use_purpose: Commit Task 1: RecordCache RWMutex (store.go + store_test.go independent)
input.command:
git add internal/monitor/store.go internal/monitor/store_test.go && git diff --cached --stat && echo '---' && git commit -m 'perf(monitor): RWMutex snapshot in RecordCache.Load to unblock concurrent readers

Move file I/O outside the lock: RLock to snapshot the cache map, run
loadRecordsWithCache unlocked, then Lock briefly to install the new map.
Concurrent dashboard + SSE readers no longer serialize on each full-cache
load. Safe because loadRecordsWithCache only reads the input map and
builds a fresh next map; fileEntry values are treated as immutable.

Adds TestRecordCacheConcurrentReads (16 goroutines x 10 loads) to guard
against races under -race.'

input.__tool_use_purpose: Commit Task 3+4 refactor
input.command:
git commit -m 'refactor(monitor): extract helpers from processRecord and finalizeSessionStats

Split two oversized aggregation functions into cohesive helpers. Behavior
unchanged; existing tests in metrics_test.go pass without modification.

processRecord (82 LoC, nesting depth 4) → 25 LoC orchestrator calling:
- touchSessionState: session lookup + cwd/end update
- recordPreToolUse: PreToolUse branch (pending enqueue, summary, skill-path)
- resolvePostToolUse: PostToolUse branch (dequeue with fallback, error split)

finalizeSessionStats (70 LoC, 3 phases) → 8 LoC orchestrator calling:
- markUnmatchedToolCalls: mark residual pending as errors
- buildSessionDetails: per-session SessionDetail construction
- foldSessionIntoAgents: agent aggregation + newest-first sort
- finalizeToolDetails (pre-existing) loop

Plus newSessionMetric pure helper shared by buildSessionDetails and
foldSessionIntoAgents.'

and on-wire format is unchanged.

Adds TestParseToolResponseError_JsonWireFormat to guard the wire format."

input.__tool_use_purpose: Commit Task 5 M4 MaxSSE clamp
input.command:
git commit -m 'fix(serve): clamp MaxSSE at math.MaxInt32 to avoid int32 wrap

opts.MaxSSE is an int; casting an oversized value to int32 wraps to a
potentially negative or small positive value, bypassing the SSE cap.
Clamp before the cast.

Adds SSEMaxForTest accessor (export_test.go) and TestNew_ClampsMaxSSE
to verify clamping and the zero-input default path.'

input.__tool_use_purpose: Stage all remaining changes for Task 2 commit
input.command: git add go.mod internal/serve/server.go internal/serve/server_test.go internal/serve/export_test.go && git diff --cached --stat && echo '---' && git stash push --keep-index -m verify-task2 && go test -race ./cmd/... ./internal/...; RC=$?; git stash pop; exit $RC

input.__tool_use_purpose: Commit Task 2 H2+H3: singleflight + loadedMetrics
input.command:
git commit -m 'perf(serve): singleflight loadMetrics and return snapshot-consistent sessions

Two bundled fixes to loadMetrics:

H2 (thundering herd): On TTL miss, N concurrent callers used to each run
AggregateDetail. Wrap the miss path with golang.org/x/sync/singleflight
so only one goroutine aggregates per TTL window; others share the result.
A double-check inside singleflight covers the race where another caller
installed a fresh entry while we waited.

H3 (snapshot consistency): handleDashboard re-read s.metricsCache.dashboardSessions
under a second metricsMu.Lock after loadMetrics returned, which could
pick up a different cache entries '\'' dashboardSessions than the dm it
already had. Change loadMetrics to return loadedMetrics{dm, sessions}
from the same cache entry; all 11 call sites updated.

Additions:
- metricsSF singleflight.Group field on Server
- aggregateDetailFn test seam + AggregateDetailFnForTest helper
- TestLoadMetricsSingleflight (20 concurrent callers, expects 1 aggregation)
- Explanatory comment on template.JS(overviewJSON): required for
  <script type="application/json"> context; string(overviewJSON) causes
  html/template to JS-stringify the JSON and break JSON.parse on the page.

golang.org/x/sync promoted from indirect to direct in go.mod.'

input.__tool_use_purpose: Final verification: all green, no unstaged changes
input.command: git status && echo '---' && git log --oneline -8 && echo '---FINAL BUILD+TEST---' && go build ./cmd/... ./internal/... && go test -race ./cmd/... ./internal/... 2>&1 | tail -5
